cnbc.title: Markets' hopes for Fed interest rate cuts are rapidly fading away
cnbc.summary: As both energy prices and inflation fears pop higher, expectations for cuts are sliding lower.

cnbc.title: Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen says he will step down after company installs successor
cnbc.summary: Shantanu Narayen led Adobe through a transition to subscription software, and he's been positioning the company to grow in the artificial intelligence age.

cnbc.title: Iran War: U.S. Navy will escort oil tankers through Strait of Hormuz when 'militarily possible,' Bessent tells Sky News
cnbc.summary: The Strait of Hormuz has been effectively closed to shipping because of the war against Iran by the U.S. and Israel.

cnbc.title: Sam Altman faced 'serious questions' in meeting with lawmakers about OpenAI's defense work
cnbc.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman met with a handful of lawmakers in Washington, D.C., to discuss the company's work with the Defense Department.

cnbc.title: Warren calls on State Department to provide more aid to Americans stuck in Middle East
cnbc.summary: The State Department said this week that 36,000 Americans had returned to the U.S. after the start of the war with Iran.

cnbc.title: Trump tariffs: Martin Heinrich bill would give families tax rebate for higher import costs
cnbc.summary: The Supreme Court in a 6-3 ruling Feb. 20 ruled that President Donald Trumps IEEPA tariffs were illegal.

cnbc.title: Palantir's technology gives the West a critical edge in Middle East, CEO Alex Karp says

cnbc.title: Why gold hasn’t moved since the Iran conflict — and where it could go next
cnbc.summary: Though the war in Iran has continued for almost two weeks, the price of the yellow metal has barely moved.

cnbc.title: Microsoft top Office executive Rajesh Jha retiring after more than 35 years
cnbc.summary: Rajesh Jha helped Microsoft bring to market an artificial intelligence assistant containing OpenAI models for its Office productivity customers.

cnbc.title: Jim Cramer: Don't let Iran war-induced market volatility scare you out of stocks
cnbc.summary: "Believe me, you'll be kicking yourself if you sell everything and then you have to watch this market rebound without you," CNBC Jim Cramer said Thursday.
